messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I haven't implemented phone calls just yet, so just SMS for now. Phone calls is something I'm still looking into but definitely hope to try and implement in future. If you hold down the user button (top one on the side) and then press the reset button briefly that should put it in DFU mode for flashing. Don't forget to format your SD card to FAT32. You can either use the Meshcore web flasher and choose the Custom Firmware option to flash the bin file- or if you wanted to have it alongside ripple on the Launcher, just copy the bin file to your SD card on your computer, then put the SD card back into your TD Pro. Then in Launcher mode on the Pro tap on the SD menu, tap the name of the firmware you want, and tap Install. If you haven't used Launcher much, fair warning that it'll say Install every time you want to swap between firmwares, regardless of whether you've installed that firmware previously. It's just a lot faster the second time around (edited)

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I very much appreciate your reply, thank you, and sorry to hear you've had a frustrating experience with the Pro! These devices have so much potential that I hope more people start developing too. There is the standalone version without BLE for maximum battery life intended for use without a companion phone app, so if you tried that firmware it won't have worked with Bluetooth or the companion app at all - in the standalone version it shouldn't even display the Bluetooth page or pin at any point. The other versions of the firmware (4G and audio BLE) do, but as I mentioned Bluetooth is disabled on boot so after flashing or resetting the device you have to manually turn it on again. I also find when pairing with the iOS app for the first time it always gives you an error (regardless of firmware) so all I do is disconnect on the app and then tap the device in the pairing list in the app and it works the second go around. With the GPS I find the first time it can be a bit finicky unless you happen to be standing outside in open air, and even then can take around a minute or so to find sufficient satellites to get a fix. No map support at the moment, unfortunately, and I'm debating whether to implement that at all given Ripple does such an excellent job with map and GPS support. The other thing I've noticed with these devices is I struggle to get anything other than a SanDisk micro SD card to work. Don't know why. The ones I have are 1TB SanDisk Ultra, but I've also tried every other size below that with success. If you haven't tried one I highly recommend giving the SanDisk SD cards a go. Thank you for your interest! It's a continuous work in progress, learning as I go. (edited)

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: If you or anyone else is interested in further testing, here's an alpha of the new 4G (no BLE) standalone version with a couple of notable differences: new Phone app screen with the option of Phone calls for non-contacts or SMS inbox where you can also call saved contacts Dialpad has three method of text entry: pressing the keys directly - so can just press w to get 1, e for 2, etc; or tap the touch screen for the numbers (although this isn't my favourite method as it currently requires tapping pretty exactly on the numbers themselves) or using the old sym+key method standalone IRC app very basic browser search using DuckDuckGo Lite Very basic html web reader browser, still very much in development , best at text-centric websites. Has epub download capability though - if you follow a link to download an epub it will save it to the books folder on your sd card, so you can read it later. Pressing X to clear cookies will also clear your history. Press K to save a bookmark when on a page. To delete bookmarks open the browser home screen, scroll down, and press delete. changed contact saving variables so you now have max 1500 contacts in the standalone variants, and can export them to SD card on the Contacts page - press C from the home screen to open Contacts then X to export, and wait 5-10 seconds (depending on contact numbers) for the popup confirmation - same with R for import contacts IMEI, carrier, and APN details displayed at the bottom of the settings menu, where your node ID and firmware version details are There's a 1400mah version for the stock battery and a 2000mah version if you've put a 2000mah battery in with a custom case (edited)
messages.content_html:
If you or anyone else is interested in further testing, here's an alpha of the new 4G (no BLE) standalone version with a couple of notable differences:
  • new Phone app screen with the option of Phone calls for non-contacts or SMS inbox where you can also call saved contacts
  • Dialpad has three method of text entry: pressing the keys directly - so can just press w to get 1, e for 2, etc; or tap the touch screen for the numbers (although this isn't my favourite method as it currently requires tapping pretty exactly on the numbers themselves) or using the old sym+key method
  • standalone IRC app
  • very basic browser search using DuckDuckGo Lite
  • Very basic html web reader browser, still very much in development, best at text-centric websites. Has epub download capability though - if you follow a link to download an epub it will save it to the books folder on your sd card, so you can read it later. Pressing X to clear cookies will also clear your history. Press K to save a bookmark when on a page. To delete bookmarks open the browser home screen, scroll down, and press delete.
  • changed contact saving variables so you now have max 1500 contacts in the standalone variants, and can export them to SD card on the Contacts page - press C from the home screen to open Contacts then X to export, and wait 5-10 seconds (depending on contact numbers) for the popup confirmation - same with R for import contacts
  • IMEI, carrier, and APN details displayed at the bottom of the settings menu, where your node ID and firmware version details are There's a 1400mah version for the stock battery and a 2000mah version if you've put a 2000mah battery in with a custom case
 (edited)

messages.text: I sure hope so! I'm particularly hoping it'll attract developers for the potential for reading texts and ebooks on the device once that backlight becomes available. I also think having audio and 4G together is potentially a bonus - it does annoy me that I can't play mp3s on the 4G version presently. With a 1tb SD card I can put my entire Audible library on there in MP3 format at the moment, for example, as well as stacks of ebooks, so it would be nice to have that as well as phone calls and SMS etc all in one. Will have to have a better read to see how they're handling power and the like and try and suss out what limitations there might be. I'm very excited for it personally and can't wait to get my hands on one so I can build Meck firmware for it and have all the features on the one device. I asked LilyGo about it yesterday and they said it may be available for sale in early April but stock will be very limited, so worth keeping an eye on their store around then

messages.text: The red LED just means your 4G modem is on. It should turn off if you go to settings and change 4G modem to off. As far as I'm aware there's no way to disable it and have 4G connectivity on at the same time. So it looks like your GPS is working if you're getting 12 sentences per second and accumulated 7033. It may just be your area - if I'm in the CBD at ground level I can't get a fix at all, for example, and if I leave GPS on while indoors it does the same thing - just accumulates sentences without getting a fix. Have you taken it outside with clear view to the sky (and away from high-rise building obstructions if you can)? If you have, and it's still not getting a fix outdoors, I'm afraid I'm baffled and confused why it would work with Ripple and not Meck

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Ripple just ignores the 4g modem as far as I can tell, as it's not built into the firmware, unfortunately. Hopefully the release of the T-Deck Pro Max will encourage Scott to incorporate it into Ripple! But you're right with Meck - hibernate actually does switch off the radio, WiFi/bluetooth etc so it's all shut down and you need to press the reboot button to turn things on again, as you've noticed. I've also just noticed the hibernate function isn't turning off the 4G modem as well as it should, will look into that. Switching the 4G modem off in the Settings menu when you're not expecting to receive or make calls etc should help with battery life otherwise. Same with turning off the WiFi modem in settings (or Bluetooth from the home page if you've got that version). Or just using hibernate as you have done. GPS uses relatively minimal power so it doesn't seem to make a huge amount of difference whether you keep that on or not. The other thing to extend battery life a little bit is changing your tx power to lower than 22. I haven't done the exact maths but it does help a little if you drop the tx power down to 20 or even 18, in terms of battery life. Other than that, I'm hoping to eventually be able to fine-tune the CPU further so it conserves power without compromising performance - I completely agree that it would be great to have a device like this as a proper phone alternative. Adding/editing contacts is something I'm working on at the moment, similar to the Discover page in Ripple where you see a list of last heard adverts and can add or remove contacts from there. It'll be out in Meck v1.2 once I've ironed out a few more bugs. And you're welcome! It's a pleasure to work on as a fun project (edited)

messages.author: Codes
messages.text: I installed v1.2-Meck-4G-BLE-merged.bin and after I figured out how to navigate settings, works pretty well, hints about navigation on first boot would be nice, they don't show up until second boot. I was able to get and send adverts, send some messages, but I haven't figured out how to read messages yet. I ran into an issue where the keyboard seemed to stop working? But it wasn't the keyboard exactly it was the main interface screen stopped responding to keyboard presses, so the whole device felt frozen. I reset the device and it didn't fix the problem. I realized that it wasn't frozen because it still updated the screen when I got a new message, and then I realized the boot button navigated between the screens and I was able to turn on BLE and GPS and use it companion mode and that worked very well. Eventually the device locked and when I unlocked it everything was working again. I don't have an SD card installed, maybe that would change things. Overall, I like it a lot, the ability to be a companion or not is excellent, I need to try to get the cellular working, overall I'm a really big fan. Can't wait to find out whats next

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Screenshot of a 4 second audio being received. The text message notification gets sent first to the contact to tell the receiver the custom audio type is on the way, then the audio packets (Direct Raw_Custom) in max 158 byte increments, so that one was a little over - 158, 158, 158, 158, 14 - plus the 38 byte pre-text, means 684 bytes in total for that 4 sec audio. I could probably squash it down further than that, but with 1200bps encoding the quality is reasonably good

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: For those interested, there is now prerelease remote repeater firmware for both the T-Deck Pro 4G and wifi versions, as well as the T5S3 Pro wifi available for testing. Please note for the wifi remote repeater environments you need to set up the /remote folder in your SD card first and include both your pre-configured mqtt.cfg and wifi.cfg files in that folder prior to flashing. (The 4G repeater only needs the mqtt.cfg file in the /remote folder.) https://github.com/pelgraine/Meck/releases/tag/test Instructions on how to set it up are available in the readme for the 4G remote repeater - https://github.com/pelgraine/Meck/blob/main/README.md I'll include the wifi version instructions later but all you need to do is create a text file, put the name of the wifi network on the first line, the password on the second line, and save it as wifi.cfg. If you have Bmorcelli's Launcher firmware already installed on your device it means you can swap back and forth between repeater and companion firmware as you please. (edited)

It’s not clear in the Lilygo docs, and they ignore a query. RE External Antenna TD Pro. Does anyone here know?
